texto: When people go away they vanish, turn to nothing, stop being. They live only in memories, haunting the imagination.
explicacao: Citação de Dezső Kosztolányi sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “When people go away they vanish, turn to nothing, stop being. They live only in memories, hauntin…” articula vida, imagination, memories em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como memories e people.

texto: At any rate, they were strange fellows, these bohemians. They lounged around doing nothing and told you they were working; they were frightfully miserable and yet would tell you that they were perfectly happy. They had more troubles than others but seemed to bear them better, as if they fed on suffering.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dezső Kosztolányi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a felicidade. O trecho “At any rate, they were strange fellows, these bohemians. They lounged around doing nothing and to…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, bohemians, suffering pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e suffering.

They lounged around doing nothing and told you they were working; they were frightfully miserable and yet would tell you that they were perfectly happy. They had more troubles than others but seemed to bear them better, as if they fed on suffering.

texto: He was no lover in a worldly sense; the only love he knew was that of divine understanding, of taking a whole life into its depths as if they were his own. From this, the greatest pain, the greatest happiness is born: the hope that we too will one day be understood, strangers will accept our words, our lives, as if they were their own.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dezső Kosztolányi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “He was no lover in a worldly sense; the only love he knew was that of divine understanding, of ta…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que espiritualidade, strangers, understanding pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de understanding.

texto: Her flesh was powdery and voluptuously weary, as if tenderized by all the different beds and arms in which she had lain. Her face was as soft as the pulpy flash of an overripe banana, her breasts like two tiny bunches of grapes. She exuded a certain seedy charm, a poetry of premature corruption and decay. She breathed the air as if it burned her palate, baking her small, hot, whorish mouth. It was as if she were sucking a sweet or slurping champagne.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dezső Kosztolányi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Her flesh was powdery and voluptuously weary, as if tenderized by all the different beds and arms…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, age, character pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e character.

texto: I,' she began in her thoughts, as we all do when thinking of ourselves. But this I was her, something, someone whose life she really lived. She was this I, in body and soul, one with its very flesh, its memories, its past, present and future, all of which we seal into a single destiny each time we face ourselves and utter that tiny, unalterable word: 'I.
